title: Recording an audio explanation
what_it_is: Voice Recorder is a free app built into Windows that records sound from your microphone and saves it as an audio file you can share.
why_use_it: A short audio recording lets an absent learner hear an explanation in your own words, without you needing to write it all out or set up a video.
how_to: Select the Start button (bottom-left of the screen), type Voice Recorder, and select the app when it appears.
Select the large microphone button in the middle of the screen to start recording.
Speak your explanation. A timer at the top of the screen shows how long you've been recording.
Select the black square button to stop the recording. It saves automatically.
To find your recording, select it from the list on the left of the Voice Recorder window, or open File Explorer and go to Documents, then Sound recordings.
To share it, right-click the recording in File Explorer, select Share, and choose Teams, email or OneDrive.

To record inside Teams or OneNote instead of using a separate app: in a Teams channel, select the paperclip icon by the message box and choose the microphone option; in OneNote, go to the Insert tab and select Record.
